questions.question: World Health Day is celebrated in the month of ________?
questions.explanation: World Health Day, celebrated on 7 April every year to mark the anniversary of the founding of WHO.

questions.question: 23rd April is celebrated as _______?
options: World Day for Safety and Health at Work
questions.explanation: 23 April is a symbolic date for world literature and celebrated as World Book and Copyright Day. Each year, UNESCO and the international organizations representing the three major sectors of the book industry - publishers, booksellers and libraries, select the World Book Capital for a one-year period, effective 23 April each year.

questions.question: Which among the following is an internationally celebrated date associated with religion?
questions.explanation: 10th may is celebrated as Vesak Day. "Vesak", the Day of the Full Moon in the month of May, is the most sacred day to millions of Buddhists around the world. It was on the Day of Vesak two and a half millennia ago, in the year 623 B.C., that the Buddha was born. It was also on the Day of Vesak that the Buddha attained enlightenment, and it was on the Day of Vesak that the Buddha in his eightieth year passed away.

questions.question: Which among the following internationally celebrated dates is related to a variety of music?
questions.explanation: In November 2011, the UNESCO General Conference declared 30 April as "International Jazz Day". The Day is intended to raise awareness in the international community of the virtues of jazz as an educational tool, and a force for peace, unity, dialogue and enhanced cooperation among people.

questions.question: June 5th is celebrated as ________?
questions.explanation: The United Nations, aware that the protection and improvement of the human environment is a major issue, which affects the well-being of peoples and economic development throughout the world, designated 5 June as the World Environment Day.

questions.question: Which among the following day is celebrated on the first Saturday of July?
questions.explanation: The United Nations' (UN) International Day of Cooperatives is observed on the first Saturday of July each year. Some of the day's goals are to increase awareness on cooperatives, as well as strengthen and extend partnerships between the international cooperative movement and other supporting organizations including governments.

questions.question: Which among the following day is celebrated on the last Thursday of September?
questions.explanation: The United Nations (UN), via the International Maritime Organization (IMO), created World Maritime Day to celebrate the international maritime industry's contribution towards the world's economy, especially in shipping. The event's date varies by year but it is always on the last Thursday of September.

questions.question: Which among the following day is NOT celebrated in the month of October?
options: World Science Day for Peace and Development
questions.explanation: Proclaimed by the UNESCO General Conference in 2001, the World Science Day for Peace and Development is an annual event celebrated on 10th November all over the world to recall the commitment made at the UNESCO-ICSU World Conference on Science. The purpose of World Science Day for Peace and Development is to renew the national, as well as the international commitment to science for peace and development and to stress the responsible use of science for the benefit of society.

questions.question: Universal Children's Day was first established in the year ______?
questions.explanation: United Nations Universal Children's Day was established in 1954 and is celebrated on November 20th each year to promote international togetherness, awareness among children worldwide, and improving children's welfare.

questions.question: International Civil Aviation Day is celebrated on 7th of ___________?
questions.explanation: It celebrated on 7th of December. The purpose of International Civil Aviation Day is to help generate and reinforce worldwide awareness of the importance of international civil aviation to the social and economic development of States, and of the unique role of ICAO in helping States to cooperate and realize a truly global rapid transit network at the service of all mankind.

questions.question: Which among the following day is celebrated once in every 5 years?
options: International Day of the Girl Child
questions.explanation: The first World Statistics Day was celebrated in 2010. The UN General Assembly decided with resolution to celebrate the day every five years. The second World Statistics Day was celebrated in 2015.

questions.question: Which among the internationally important days was NOT an initiative of UNESCO?
questions.explanation: Every April 26, World Intellectual Property Organization promotes celebration of World Intellectual Property Day to learn about the role that intellectual property rights (patents, trademarks, industrial designs, copyright) play in encouraging innovation and creativity.

questions.question: On which month a day is dedicated for a species of a fish?
questions.explanation: World Tuna Day is celebrated on 2nd May. World Tuna Day is to highlight the importance of sustainably managed fish stocks in achieving the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

questions.question: Which among the following days doesn't fall on 30th of any month?
options: World Day against Trafficking in Persons
questions.explanation: The United Nations' (UN) International Human Solidarity Day is annually held on December 20 to celebrate unity in diversity. It also aims to remind people on the importance of solidarity in working towards eradicating poverty.

questions.question: September 5th is celebrated by UN as __________?
questions.explanation: The International Day of Charity is observed annually on 5 September. It was declared by the United Nations General Assembly in 2012. The prime purpose of the International Day of Charity is to raise awareness and provide a common platform for charity related activities all over the world for individuals, charitable, philanthropic and volunteer organizations for their own purposes on the local, national, regional and international level.

questions.question: 11th December is designated as _________?
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly designated 11 December "International Mountain Day". As of 2003, it has been observed every year to create awareness about the importance of mountains to life, to highlight the opportunities and constraints in mountain development and to build alliances that will bring positive change to mountain peoples and environments around the world.

questions.question: International Day of Women and Girls in Science is celebrated on 11th of ____?
questions.explanation: In order to achieve full and equal access to and participation in science for women and girls, and further achieve gender equality and the empowerment of women and girls, the United Nations General Assembly adopted resolution declaring 11 February as the International Day of Women and Girls in Science.

questions.question: In India, May 30th is celebrated as ___________?
questions.explanation: The Hindi Journalism Day is celebrated every year on May 30 in India to mark the beginning to journalism in Hindi language. Hindi journalism was born in Kolkata. A weekly newspaper, called Udant Martand was started.

questions.question: National Safe Motherhood Day is celebrated on ___ of April.
questions.explanation: National Safe Motherhood Day is observed on April 11 every year. National Safe Motherhood Day is an initiative of the White Ribbon Alliance India, to enforce that women must have the availability and adequate access to care during pregnancy, childbirth and postnatal services.

questions.question: Which among the following days is observed on second Monday of March?
questions.explanation: Commonwealth Day is the annual celebration of the Commonwealth of Nations held on the second Monday in March.

questions.question: World Immunization Day is observed during the last week of __________?
questions.explanation: World Immunization Week is a global public health campaign to raise awareness and increase rates of immunization against vaccine-preventable diseases around the world. It takes place each year during last week of April.

questions.question: UN has designated May 29th as _________?
options: International Day of UN Peace Keepers
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly designated 29 May as the International Day of United Nations Peacekeepers. UN Peacekeeping operations use the Day to strengthen bonds with the local populations that they have been deployed to serve.

questions.question: In India, teacher's day is celebrated in memory of _________?
questions.explanation: In India Teacher's Day is celebrated on 5th of September as a tribute to the contribution made by the teachers towards the community. Since 1962 the day commemorates the birthday of Dr Sarvepalli Radhakhrishnan, a great teacher and a staunch believer of education, known for his contribution towards the education system of India.

questions.question: World Sight day is celebrated on second __________ of October.
questions.explanation: World Sight Day (WSD) is an annual day of awareness held on the second Thursday of October, to focus global attention on blindness and vision impairment.

questions.question: Third Sunday of November is observed as ________?
options: World Day of Remembrance for Road Traffic Victims
International Day for the Abolition of Slavery
International Volunteer Day for Economic and Social Development
questions.explanation: World Day of Remembrance for Road Traffic Victims is observed on third Sunday of November. The Day has become an important tool in global efforts to reduce road casualties. It offers an opportunity for drawing attention to the scale of emotional and economic devastation caused by road crashes and for giving recognition to the suffering of road crash victims and the work of support and rescue services.

questions.question: International Anti-Corruption Day falls in the month of ________
questions.explanation: International Anti-Corruption Day has been observed annually, on 9 December. It aims to raise public awareness of corruption and what people can do to fight it.

questions.question: World Day for International Justice of observed in the month of ______.
questions.explanation: World Day for International Justice, also referred to as Day of International Criminal Justice or International Justice Day, is an international day celebrated throughout the world on July 17 as part of an effort to recognize the emerging system of international criminal justice.

questions.question: 27th of July is the foundation day of which among the following Indian armed forces?
questions.explanation: The Central Reserve Police Force came into existence as Crown Representative's Police on 27th July 1939, which is observed as foundation day. It became the Central Reserve Police Force on enactment of the CRPF Act on 28th December 1949.

questions.question: International Migrants Day is observed on ___ of December.
questions.explanation: The United Nations (UN) International Migrants Day is annually held on December 18 to recognize the efforts, contributions, and rights of migrants worldwide.

questions.question: World Water Day is celebrated on 22nd of ________?
questions.explanation: World Water Day is held annually on 22 March as a means of focusing attention on the importance of freshwater and advocating for the sustainable management of freshwater resources.
